title: hippocrepis comosa var. commutata
intro: Hippocrepis comosa var. commutata, also known as the hairy needle weed, is a plant species that belongs to the Fabaceae family, characterized by its hairy stems and needle-like leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting the immune system. Hippocrepis comosa var. commutata is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be found in some herbal supplements, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential interactions with other medications.
